commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: No automated repair is indicated; the remaining decision is maintainer acceptance of the cancellation contract, companion PR relationship, and proof scope.; Cleared: The diff touches TypeScript CLI/context-engine source and tests only, with no dependency, workflow, credential, or package-execution surface changes. (sha=98802c0f63c0a4fcc8639b8dde535f3bed879207)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds full-CLI teardown that cancels queued or active deferred context-engine turn maintenance on completion, threads an abort signal into maintenance runtime context, resets affected command lanes, and adds focused CLI and maintenance tests.

PR surface: Source +154, Tests +412. Total +566 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main can leave active deferred turn-maintenance work and its progress timer alive after the foreground CLI command completes. I did not run a live current-main reproduction in this read-only review, but the PR body supplies after-fix terminal proof.
commands.review_followup: No automated repair is indicated; the remaining decision is maintainer acceptance of the cancellation contract, companion PR relationship, and proof scope.

target.title: fix(cli): cancel deferred maintenance on exit
target.body:
## Problem

Some short-lived `openclaw agent --local` commands can finish their foreground work while background context-engine maintenance is still queued or running. In that state the command has no more user-visible work to do, but deferred maintenance can keep the Node process alive and make operators wait for an outer wrapper timeout.

## Change and value

This PR adds a targeted CLI teardown step for full Commander-based CLI commands. After `program.parseAsync()` returns, the CLI cancels active deferred turn-maintenance runs, clears their queue lanes, and gives them a short best-effort drain before the rest of process cleanup continues.

The value is narrower than a global exit workaround: completed short-lived CLI commands can release known deferred context-engine work without calling `process.exit()` or changing routed fast-path commands.

## Who is affected, and who is not

- Affected: operators and scripts running short-lived full CLI commands, especially `openclaw agent --local`, where deferred context-engine maintenance can otherwise keep the process alive after command completion.
- Not affected: routed fast-path commands such as the lightweight `status` path. Those do not enter the full Commander parse path and do not run this cancellation hook.
- Not fixed here: cases where the agent command never resolves or its own timeout does not abort the run. That is covered separately by #86276.

## Implementation

- Adds `runtimeContext.abortSignal` for host-owned best-effort cancellation of background context-engine maintenance while keeping the public top-level `ContextEngine.maintain()` parameter shape stable.
- Tracks active deferred turn-maintenance task/run ids so CLI shutdown can mark queued or active work as cancelled instead of letting stale workers overwrite terminal task state.
- Clears and resets the affected turn-maintenance command lanes during CLI completion.
- Fences runtime-owned transcript rewrite helpers after abort so a cancelled maintenance task cannot rewrite the transcript after CLI completion.
- Clears and unreferences the long-running progress timer when shutdown cancellation fires, including the case where a plugin ignores the abort signal and never resolves.
- Drops coalesced rerun requests once CLI shutdown cancellation starts.
- Runs cancellation only after full CLI parsing was entered, so routed commands keep their existing lightweight behavior.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: completed short-lived CLI commands can be held open by queued or active deferred context-engine maintenance after user-visible work finishes.

Real environment tested: Linux/WSL local OpenClaw source setup. Standalone #86264 source head was `98802c0f63c0a4fcc8639b8dde535f3bed879207`. Combined stack proof used #86264 `98802c0f63c0a4fcc8639b8dde535f3bed879207` plus #86276 `e0e10b389dc3c08b27890db5d78e47d3fc9ef58f`, combined head `6a806f8f19628c16f99627162b8f53a01e27e680`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
timeout 240s node /tmp/openclaw-cli-exit-combined/openclaw.mjs agent --local --agent main --session-id combined-cli-exit-proof --message 'Return marker OPENCLAW_CLI_EXIT_COMBINED_PROOF_OK' --thinking off --json
```

Evidence after fix: Terminal transcript, embedded-agent JSON output, and mock-server request log were captured under `/home/captain/codex-workspace/proof/2026-05-25-cli-exit-combined/`, including `32-after-combined-source-mock-success.out`, `32-after-combined-source-mock-success.agent.jsonl`, and `32-after-combined-source-mock-success.mock-requests.jsonl`.

```text
REPO_HEAD 6a806f8f19628c16f99627162b8f53a01e27e680
COMMAND timeout 240s node /tmp/openclaw-cli-exit-combined/openclaw.mjs agent --local --agent main --session-id combined-cli-exit-proof --message Return marker OPENCLAW_CLI_EXIT_COMBINED_PROOF_OK --thinking off --json
finalAssistantVisibleText: OPENCLAW_CLI_EXIT_COMBINED_PROOF_OK
executionTrace.winnerProvider: openai
executionTrace.winnerModel: gpt-5.5
executionTrace.attempts[0].result: success
executionTrace.runner: embedded
mock request path: /v1/responses
EXIT_CODE=0
ELAPSED_SEC=41
ASSERT_AGENT_TURN=passed
```

Observed result after fix: The combined `agent --local` turn emitted a final embedded-agent result, used the configured OpenAI-compatible endpoint, and exited normally with code `0` in 41 seconds, well before the outer `timeout 240s` wrapper. This closes the prior result-plus-exit proof gap for the combined CLI-exit solution.

Before evidence optional: Additional boundary proof from #86276 on the same combined stack showed the companion no-completion fix also composes correctly.

```text
local agent command timed out after 1s plus 30s grace
EXIT_CODE=124
ELAPSED_SEC=39
```

What was not tested: a standalone built-package exact-head #86264 proof. The local #86264 worktree initially lacked `dist/entry`, and local build attempts stalled in pnpm/tsdown dependency/build setup before a standalone source proof could run. This PR should remain draft until maintainers accept the combined-stack proof or a clean standalone CI/package proof is added.

## Verification

Previously completed on standalone #86264 head `98802c0f63c0a4fcc8639b8dde535f3bed879207`:

- Maintenance tests: 24/24
- CLI exit tests: 89/89
- `oxfmt --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `tsgo:core` via `scripts/run-tsgo.mjs`
- clean merge-tree against latest fetched `origin/main`
- required Codex review against `ec168fa2bdda15be7430f41cb7ef0b610b6e68bf`: no actionable correctness issues

Rechecked on combined stack `6a806f8f19628c16f99627162b8f53a01e27e680`:

- `pnpm build`
- `pnpm test:unit:fast -- src/cli/run-main.test.ts src/cli/run-main.exit.test.ts src/commands/agent-via-gateway.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/context-engine-maintenance.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/cli/run-main.ts src/cli/run-main.test.ts src/cli/run-main.exit.test.ts src/commands/agent-via-gateway.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/context-engine-maintenance.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`

## Relationship to #86276

#86276 handles the no-completion class: a local/embedded agent command never settles, so the CLI needs a hard wall-clock boundary. This PR handles the post-completion cleanup class: a command has produced its result and needs scoped cleanup of known deferred maintenance work so the process can exit. The combined proof verifies the two fixes work together.

AI-assisted: yes.
target.author: kaspre

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
